name: shared_end_label
title: Defeat GCC constant-prop in switch-with-return via single shared end-label
summary: Switch-with-return where each case sets a return variable and explicitly returns: if at least one case always returns 0 (or another constant matching the var's natural initial value), GCC constant-folds `return s2;` to `return 0;` and dead-store-eliminates the `s2 = 0;` instruction. Restructure with `goto end; ... end: return s2;` so GCC sees s2 as potentially varying across cases.

structural: Switch with 2+ cases, each ending with `return <var>;`
At least one case always returns 0 (the constant-fold trigger)
Function is 1-N instructions short, specifically missing `addu $sN, $0, $zero` at case heads
OR: regfix has `insert_after "addu $sN, $0, $zero" @ idx` rules restoring the missing inits

rule_skeleton.before: case N: ...; s2 = K; return s2;
rule_skeleton.after: case N: ...; s2 = K; goto end;

rule_skeleton.comment: Bounds-check also gotos end (matches target's $s2-from-entry return)

notes: Reference: func_80077B30 (commit 0f206e5). Switch on D_800A35E4 with cases 0-5; cases 1 and 5 always return 0. Restructure removed 2 insert_after cheats AND all 17 register/label substs — became 100% pure C with zero regfix rules.
Does NOT apply to single-case or 2-case switches (different GCC optimization landscape).
Does NOT apply when cases return through different exit paths (not all going to same end).
Try this FIRST when you see `lost_codegen` audit warnings or a function 1-2 instructions short with missing `addu $sN, $0, $zero` at case heads.

If the bounds-check failure path returns a literal (not the return variable), changing it to `goto end` matches GCC's natural treatment of an uninitialized callee-save register at the shared exit — that pattern is common in target.s and worth replicating.
